attempt: Transfer TICKET46's rule: a finite symbolic grammar that only works by adding a horizon/depth escape coordinate cannot be promoted to a proof. It must either become a compact invariant theorem or produce a genuine counterexample.

bounded_result.discarded_shortcut: finite exact-gap selector repaired by range-dependent leakage coordinates
bounded_result.retained_target: A compact gap-2 selector or Maynard-type weight whose positive mass survives every range extension with fixed parameters.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a leakage pressure cycle where wider gaps absorb every fixed exact-gap selector margin

obstruction: The project has improved the falsification protocol for proposed proof grammars, but this transfer is not an infinite proof and not a certified counterexample.
candidate_theorem: A compact gap-2 selector or Maynard-type weight whose positive mass survives every range extension with fixed parameters.
next_experiment: Audit range-dependent selector repairs and extract the first fixed-parameter leakage cycle.
claim_boundary: No twin-prime proof and no certified twin-prime counterexample.
